summary.intent: Introduce zizmor (GitHub Actions static analysis tool) to the project, configured via lefthook pre-commit hook and a dedicated CI workflow, while also removing merge_group triggers from existing workflows.
summary.outcome: Successfully introduced zizmor 1.24.1 via .tool-versions, added a pre-commit lefthook step, created a new zizmor.yaml CI workflow using the official zizmorcore/zizmor-action pinned by SHA, fixed template-injection findings in existing workflows, and committed all changes.

repo: Project uses lefthook for git hooks, mise (.tool-versions) for tool version management, and has no task runner — zizmor is invoked directly.
Existing workflows (commitlint.yaml, validate-plugin.yaml) had merge_group triggers that were intentionally removed per user request.
commitlint.yaml used ${{ github.event.pull_request.*.sha }} directly in run: steps — a template-injection vulnerability that needed env: indirection.
No persist-credentials: false was set on checkout steps in existing workflows.

code.finding: PR SHA references in run: steps must go through env: vars to prevent template injection; merge_group trigger removed.
Added persist-credentials: false to checkout action; removed merge_group trigger.
New workflow: uses top-level permissions: {} with per-job minimal grants; zizmorcore/zizmor-action pinned by SHA for supply-chain safety.
Initial attempt used bare zizmor command; required mise exec -- zizmor because mise-managed tools aren't on PATH before mise shell activation in hook context.

workflow: zizmor's official action (zizmorcore/zizmor-action) should be pinned by SHA, not tag, for supply-chain safety.
When using mise-managed tools in lefthook hooks, wrap commands with mise exec -- <tool> to ensure the tool is on PATH regardless of shell activation state.
zizmor findings must be resolved in all workflow files before the zizmor CI check itself can pass — order of operations matters when self-hosting a linter that checks the CI that runs it.

friction: lefthook pre-commit hook failed on first commit because mise-managed zizmor was not on PATH — required amending the hook to use mise exec -- zizmor.
zizmor was not already installed locally, requiring mise install before being able to verify findings interactively.
open_items: Each developer needs to run mise install to get zizmor locally before the pre-commit hook will work for them.
PR creation was staged in the task list but the transcript ends at commit — PR may not have been created yet.
